programs.programName: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE)

verified.notes: No income or asset limits found in sources; PACE typically uses Medicaid eligibility which has no countable asset limit for aged/disabled in most states. Multiple provider phones listed but no single official main phone.

programs.programName: Senior Health Insurance Information Program (SHIIP) — Iowa's implementation of the national SHIP program

verified.notes: SHIIP is a free counseling service for Medicare beneficiaries with no age, income, or asset eligibility tests

programs.notes: SHIIP is a free counseling service for Medicare beneficiaries with no age, income, or asset eligibility tests

verified.notes: Search results contain limited program details for Iowa Meals on Wheels. Two Iowa providers identified (WesleyLife in Central Iowa and NEI3A covering 18 counties), but neither search result provides minimum age requirement, income limits, asset limits, or official phone numbers. Age 60 is standard for Older Americans Act programs nationally, but cannot be confirmed from provided Iowa-specific sources. Recommend contacting Iowa Department of Elder Affairs or individual local providers directly for current eligibility and application details.
